summary:
Current status: 14 states have filed bills restricting data center construction. 11 states have legislation under committee review. All 25 bills remain in committee — none have advanced to a floor vote.

Key bills to watch:
• Virginia HB 1515 — Moratorium until July 2028. Highest-impact bill given Virginia hosts the world's largest data center market.
• Maine LD 307 — Ban on facilities over 20 MW until Nov 2027. Would be the first state-level capacity-based ban if enacted.
• New York S9144 — 3-year moratorium. Builds on the state's 2022 crypto mining moratorium (S6486D, enacted).
• Vermont H. 289 — Freeze until July 2030, the longest proposed moratorium period nationally.
• Oklahoma SB 1488 — Moratorium until Nov 2029, citing water resource concerns.

Common legislative triggers across states: grid capacity strain, water resource competition, and lack of local revenue-sharing frameworks. Indiana's HB 1333 is a notable outlier — it requires revenue-sharing with local governments rather than imposing a construction freeze.

18 states maintain active incentive programs. Major incentive markets (TX, IA, NV, AZ) have not introduced restriction legislation. No state has enacted a broad data center moratorium or ban to date.
